meaning: adj.投下的,举手过肩而投掷的adv.举手过肩地,手势向下地vt.重复缝纫,锁边n.手举过肩地游泳姿势
examples: Ball may be served underhand or overhand.
To sew with a loose overcast or overhand stitch.
Brutal and unforeseen events take overhand and build a bridge to the past.
Fortuna swarmed Zamudio, from Mexico, in the opening seconds of the scheduled 12-rounder and dropped him with an overhand left.
It won't be long before this muscle mania reaches Broadway and we get a Willy Loman who yells at Biff while ripping through sets of overhand pull-ups.
